Assembly: The assembly of Metafruticicola noverca (xgMetNove1) is based on 63X PacBio data and 29X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the European Reference Genome Atlas (ERGA, https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/) via the Biodiversity Genomics Europe project (BGE, https://biodiversitygenomics.eu/). The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 35 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial), totaling 1.8 Mb (with the largest being 647 Kb). Additionally, 11,422 regions totaling 2.14 Gb (with the largest being 5.1 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 659 haplotypic regions were removed, totaling 355.2 Mb (with the largest being 38.3 Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly ilCyaSemi1.1 is based on 42x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHifi. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly ilEuxTeme1.hap1.1 is based on 45x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4). A haplotypic inversion was observed in the region on chromosome 5 (18.8 -29.2 Mbp). Region on chromosome 6 (12.6-14.2 Mbp) is collapsed on hap2.

Assembly: The assembly icAgoVidu2.1 is based on 63x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. A manually phased assembly (based on HiC signal) was generated from a jointly curated primary and alt assembly. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. X chromosome identified based on BUSCO gene painting with ancestral coleopteran linkage groups and copy number in the diploid assembly. Chromosome Y was identified by copy number in the diploid assembly. Inversions between haplotypes were observed in the following approximate locations: Chromosome 1 42.60-44.42Mb Chromosome 2 17.61-20.18Mb Chromosome 11 2.42-8.45Mb Chromosome 12 1.24-4.05Mb The order and orientation of the following regions is uncertain: Chromosome 3 13.95-15.21Mb Chromosome 15 3.91-7.43Mb Chromosome 16 0.78-5.33Mb.

Assembly: 'Artema nephilit was assembled with Hifiasm (version 0.25.0-r726). The consensus assembly was checked for contaminants using FCS-GX (version 0.5.5) based on the ALL database released on 2023/01, and purged for haplotypic duplication using purge_dups (version 1.2.5). Yahs (version 1.2a.1) was used to scaffold contigs into chromosomes. The manual curation was done with Sanger-tol/curationpretext (version 1.5), PretextView (version 1.0.4) and Sanger-tol/agp-tpf-utils (commit: 35ea45dbcd8f3ece7a91104e5813541b55beb2ff). The mitochondrial genome could not be assembled using MitoHiFi (3.2.3). The sex system is X1X2 but could not be assigned through synteny.'.

Assembly: This project presents the alternate haplotype of the diploid genome assembly of the garden speedwell, Veronica longifolia, from a sample provided by Thomas Marcussen. The data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). The daVerLong1.1 assembly is based on 32x PacBio HiFi and 60x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt and assembled with hifiasm together with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies, ensuring that reads for hap1 scaffolding did not contain k-mers unique to hap2, and vice versa. YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes, as confirmed by Hi-C data, are named according to size.

Assembly: The assembly ilPenMori1.hap1.1 is based on 210x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosomes Z and W were identified by copy number in the diploid assembly. The order and orientation of scaffolds making up the W chromosomes is uncertain. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 18 (2.5 - 3.2 and 10.1 - 10.6 Mbp) are unknown.
